entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCES
entities.description: Natural assets of a region that influence the selection and arrangement of crops

entities.name: LEVEL OF TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The degree of technological application in farming practices

entities.name: RELATIVE COMMODITY PRICES
entities.description: The price of one agricultural product compared to others, affecting profitability

entities.name: MARKET INFRASTRUCTURE
entities.description: The physical and organizational structures needed for the sale and distribution of crops

entities.name: CROPPING PATTERN
entities.description: The specific arrangement and sequence of crops grown in a particular area

entities.name: LAND
entities.description: A primary farm resource whose utilization is typically determined by ownership

entities.name: LABOR
entities.description: A farm resource where accessibility and use are not strictly determined by ownership

entities.name: BULLOCKS
entities.description: Draft animals used for farm work and transportation

entities.name: FARM EQUIPMENT
entities.description: Tools and machinery used for agricultural production

entities.name: CANAL IRRIGATION
entities.description: The method of supplying water to crops through a system of man-made canals

entities.name: TRACTORIZATION
entities.description: The process of introducing and using tractors for farm operations

entities.name: CROSS SECTIONAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: A research method used to analyze differences across various resource levels

entities.name: LAND TYPES
entities.description: Different classifications of land based on physical and chemical properties

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The artificial application of water to soil to assist in crop growth

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: The amount of precipitation received, acting as a natural water source for crops

entities.name: CROP VARIETIES
entities.description: Different cultivars of crops that can offer different advantages

entities.name: MARKETS
entities.description: Systems and locations where agricultural commodities are bought and sold

entities.name: COMPARATIVE ADVANTAGE
entities.description: The ability of a specific land or farm to produce certain crops more efficiently than others

entities.name: SOIL TYPES
entities.description: Different categories of soil that affect crop suitability and yield

entities.name: RATE OF RETURN TO INVESTMENT
entities.description: The financial gain relative to the cost of improving the resource base

entities.name: HIGH VALUE CROPS
entities.description: Crops that provide higher economic returns per unit of land

entities.name: MIXED CROPS
entities.description: A farming system where multiple crop species are grown together
